claims.statement: The compiler pipeline is deterministic except for a single bounded LLM call per dirty page
Isidore detects claim-level staleness without any LLM calls
Claim hashes are computed as a SHA‑256 over the cited line ±2 lines, whitespace‑normalized, then truncated to 12 hex characters
The structure graph format is a JSON object with "nodes" and "links" (or "edges") arrays
The CLI 'claims' subcommand performs a zero‑LLM staleness audit and can exit with code 1 if any claim is stale
Findings are side observations collected during compilation with near‑zero marginal cost
module docstring describes claims parsing, anchoring/quarantine, and zero‑LLM staleness property
module imports anchor_claims, check_claims, claim_id, evidence_hash, evidence_state, parse_claims_block, render_claims, stale_pages from isidore.claims
_make_repo creates 12 source files each with 10 lines and a graph.json file in a graphify-out directory
test_parse_claims_block_extracts_and_stips verifies that the cleaned page no longer contains "isidore-claims" and still contains "Fine page."
test_evidence_hash_is_the_cited_line_content confirms evidence_hash returns a deterministic 12‑character hash for a valid citation and None for missing files or out‑of‑range lines

test_evidence_state_ignores_neighbors_whitespace_and_line_shifts confirms that changing a neighboring line, re‑indenting, or inserting lines above does not mark a citation stale, but modifying the cited line marks it stale and deleting the file marks it orphan

findings.note: test_anchor_claims_quarantines_ghost_paths is declared but its implementation is not present in the excerpts, leaving behavior unverified

claims.statement: _node returns a dict with keys "id", "source_file", "file_type", "label", and "source_location".
_link returns a dict with keys "source", "target", and "relation".
_make_repo writes a JSON file named "graph.json" under "graphify-out".
_graph reads "graph.json" and returns the contained "nodes" and "links".
_gp returns the path to "graph.json" inside the repository.
test_plan_pages_selects_top_modules_excluding_small_and_concepts asserts that plan_pages returns specs named "mod0/core", "mod1/core", and "mod2/core".
test_plan_pages_top_k_and_none_means_all asserts that top_k=2 yields two specs and top_k=None yields three specs.
test_plan_pages_records_cross_module_deps is defined to test cross‑module dependency recording.

findings.note: The body of test_plan_pages_records_cross_module_deps is not shown; it is unclear what assertions it makes or whether it is fully implemented.

claims.statement: encode_table("t", ["a","b"], [{"a":"x,y","b":None},{"a":"","b":True}]) produces the lines ['t[2]{a,b}:','  "x,y",','  ,1']
module_of("a/b/c.py", 2) returns "a/b"
scan_repo(tmp_path) yields node labels that include "alpha.py", "beta.py", "top()", "Thing", "helper()", and "notes.md"
scan_repo(tmp_path) never produces nodes whose source_file path contains ".venv"
write_scan(tmp_path) returns a path such that find_graph(tmp_path) equals that path
parse_findings_block(md) strips the ``isidore-findings`` fence and extracts findings kinds ["bug","drift","question"]
